description: Create the complete CloudVault desktop GUI application using Python + CustomTkinter with a purple (#8B5CF6) and jet black (#0a0a0a) theme, cloud icon, system tray integration, and all management screens wired to the existing PowerShell scripts.
steps: Create gui/ directory structure: gui/app.py (main entry), gui/core/__init__.py, gui/core/theme.py (color constants and theme config), gui/core/script_runner.py (PowerShell subprocess integration), gui/core/settings_manager.py (read/write config/settings.json), gui/core/status_monitor.py (periodic health polling)

Create gui/assets/ directory with a programmatically-generated cloud icon: gui/assets/generate_icon.py that creates cloud.ico (32x32, 48x48, 256x256 sizes) using Pillow with purple (#8B5CF6) cloud shape on transparent background

Create gui/views/__init__.py and implement gui/views/dashboard.py - main dashboard frame showing: service status (Docker, Immich, containers), disk usage bar (purple themed), last sync time, cloud storage summary, quick action buttons (Start/Stop/Sync)

Create gui/views/sync_view.py - sync management frame with: manual sync button (calls sync-cloud.ps1), force sync button (calls sync-cloud.ps1 -Force), dry-run toggle, sync progress display, last sync log viewer, sync schedule configuration (calls schedule-sync.ps1)

Create gui/views/clouds_view.py - cloud providers management frame showing: configured providers (Mega, Google Drive, OneDrive) with status indicators, capacity bars for each provider, button to reconfigure (calls configure-cloud.ps1), button to verify connections

Create gui/views/settings_view.py - settings editor frame that: reads config/settings.json, allows editing key settings (sync schedule, bandwidth limit, storage paths, auto-cleanup toggles, port, dry_run mode), saves changes back to settings.json with validation
Create gui/views/logs_view.py - log viewer frame with: real-time log file display from logs/ directory, log level filter (INFO/WARN/ERROR), auto-scroll, clear button, export button

Create gui/views/restore_view.py - restore management frame with: provider selection dropdown, destination path chooser, list files button (calls restore.ps1 -ListOnly), restore button with confirmation dialog

Create gui/app.py - main application class using CustomTkinter with: CTk window (title 'CloudVault', cloud icon), sidebar navigation (Dashboard, Sync, Clouds, Settings, Logs, Restore), frame switching, window minimize to system tray (using pystray), startup status check

Create gui/tray.py - system tray integration using pystray with: cloud icon in system tray, right-click menu (Show, Start Services, Stop Services, Sync Now, Exit), double-click to restore window, status tooltip
Create gui/requirements.txt with: customtkinter>=5.2.0, Pillow>=10.0.0, pystray>=0.19.0, darkdetect>=0.8.0
Create gui/build.exe.ps1 - PyInstaller build script that: installs pyinstaller, runs pyinstaller with --onefile --windowed --icon=assets/cloud.ico --name=CloudVault, outputs to dist/CloudVault.exe
Create gui/README.md with installation and usage instructions in Spanish
Update the root .gitignore to add gui/__pycache__/, gui/dist/, gui/build/, gui/*.spec, gui/assets/cloud.ico (generated file)
Update the root README.md to add a section about the GUI application with screenshot placeholder and instructions to run it
acceptance_criteria: All Python files in gui/ pass syntax validation via ast.parse()
gui/app.py is the main entry point that creates the CustomTkinter window
Theme uses purple (#8B5CF6) and jet black (#0a0a0a) throughout all views
Cloud icon is generated programmatically via gui/assets/generate_icon.py
System tray integration is implemented in gui/tray.py using pystray
All 6 views (Dashboard, Sync, Clouds, Settings, Logs, Restore) are implemented with full UI
PowerShell script execution is handled through gui/core/script_runner.py using subprocess
Settings read/write uses the existing config/settings.json format
gui/build.exe.ps1 provides a one-command build to .exe
verification: Run: find gui/ -name '*.py' -exec python3 -c "import ast,sys; ast.parse(open(sys.argv[1]).read()); print(f'OK: {sys.argv[1]}')" {} \; to verify all Python files have valid syntax
Verify that gui/core/script_runner.py references the correct PowerShell script paths (scripts/start-services.ps1, scripts/stop-services.ps1, etc.)
Verify that gui/core/settings_manager.py reads from config/settings.json path
Verify that gui/requirements.txt exists with customtkinter, Pillow, pystray listed
findings: All 15 Python files pass AST syntax validation. Complete GUI application implemented with all 6 views, system tray, theme constants, PowerShell script integration, settings management, and status monitoring. Root .gitignore and README.md updated. Post-review fixes applied: WM_DELETE_WINDOW typo corrected, command injection sanitization added, thread-safe callbacks via copy-on-iterate, cancellable sync subprocess, interactive script terminal invocation, and settings validation rules.
